---
lang: en

title: "Analysis of Hannah Montana Viewership Data"
subtitle: "Reproducible Research --- Assignment 11"
author:
  name:
    given: "Levente Laszlo"
    family: "Szabo"
    literal: "Levente Laszlo Szabo (488761)"
  email: "ll.szabo@student.uw.edu.pl"
  affiliation:
    name: "University of Warsaw"
    department: "Data Science and Business Analytics"
    group: "Master's Degree"
    address: "ul. Krakowskie Przedmiescie 26/28"
    city: "Warsaw"
    country: "Poland"
    postal-code: "00-927"
    url: "https://en.uw.edu.pl"
date: "today"

pagetitle: "Assignment 11 --- Levente Szabo"
author-meta: "Levente Laszlo Szabo (488761)"
date-meta: "`{r} Sys.Date()`"

format:
  html:
    theme: "zephyr"
    mainfont: "Times New Roman"
    fontsize: "1.1em"
    linestretch: 1.5

    page-layout: "full"
    date-format: "long"
    number-sections: true
    anchor-sections: false

    toc: true
    toc-title: "Table of Contents"
    toc-location: "left"
    toc-expand: true
    toc-depth: 3

    highlight-style: "github"
    code-line-numbers: true

    embed-resources: true
    progress: false
    smooth-scroll: true

bibliography: "references.bib"
bibliographystyle: "apa"
csl: apa.csl
biblio-title: "Works Cited"

editor: source
---

```{r settingUp}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
#| error: false
#| warning: false

Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "en_US.UTF-8")

options(
  width = 130
)

library(tidyverse)

data <- read_tsv(
  file = "C:\\Users\\SzabónéTátraiKatalin\\Documents\\University of Milan\\OneDrive - Università degli Studi di Milano\\Subjects\\University of Warsaw\\2nd semester\\Reproducible Research\\Seminar 11\\Dataset.tsv",
  col_names = TRUE,
  col_types = "nncccDn",
  na = "N/A",
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  skip_empty_rows = TRUE,
  progress = FALSE
)
```




## Introduction

My dataset [@panamitsu__2026] stores an overview of the episodes of **Hannah Montana**. My goal in this assignment is to analyze the viewership of the TV show over time.



### About the Series of Hannah Montana

**Hannah Montana** is a teen sitcom that follows *Miley Stewart*, a regular girl living a double life as a famous pop star named *Hannah Montana*. By day, she is a normal student dealing with school, friends, and family. By night, she performs as *Hannah Montana*, hiding her true identity from the public to maintain a normal life. Only her close friends and family know her secret. \
The show mixes comedy, music, and coming-of-age themes, focusing on balancing fame with personal life, staying true to yourself, and handling typical teenage challenges. It stars *Miley Cyrus* and played a big role in Disney Channel’s popularity in the late $2000$s.



### Poster of Hannah Montana

![&#128279; @tiansw__2026](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/I/71OBvF8twfL._AC_SL1046_.jpg){width=250}




## Analysis



### Summary of the Viewership

```{r summary}
#| echo: true
#| output: true
#| error: false
#| warning: false

summary(data$viewers)
```



### Graph of the Viewership Over Time

```{r timeSeries}
#| echo: false
#| output: true
#| error: false
#| warning: false

ggplot(
  data = data,
  aes(
    x = release,
    y = viewers
  )
) + geom_line(
  color = "#1A85FF",
  linewidth = 1.3,
  show.legend = FALSE
) + scale_y_continuous(
  limits = c(0, 12),
  breaks = seq(0, 12, by = 2),
  expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0))
) + labs(
  title = "Viewership Over Time",
  subtitle = "viewership for some episodes is unknown",
  x = NULL,
  y = "viewership (million)"
) + theme_bw(
  base_size = 18
) + theme(
  legend.position = "none",
  plot.title = element_text(
    size = 22,
    face = "bold"
  ),
  plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 20),
  axis.title = element_text(size = 15)
)
```



### Graph of the Season-to-Season Changes in Viewership

```{r changes}
#| echo: false
#| output: true
#| error: false
#| warning: false

data_season <- data %>%
  drop_na(viewers) %>%
    group_by(season) %>%
      summarise(
        mean = mean(viewers),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
        arrange(season) %>%
          mutate(
            change = mean - lag(mean)
          ) %>%
            filter(
              season > 1
            )

ggplot(
  data = data_season,
  aes(
    x = season,
    y = change
  )
) + geom_col(
  position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
  width = 0.7,
  color = "#39E600",
  fill = "#339933"
) + geom_text(
  aes(
    label = paste0(
      ifelse(change > 0, "+", ""),
      round(change, 3),
      " million"
    )
  ),
  position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
  vjust = -0.5,
  size = 5,
  fontface = "bold",
  color = "#39E600",
  lineheight = 1
) + scale_x_continuous(
  limits = c(1.5, 4.5),
  breaks = seq(2, 4, by = 1),
  expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0))
) + scale_y_continuous(
  limits = c(-1, 1),
  breaks = seq(-1, 1, by = 0.25),
  expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0))
) + labs(
  title = "Viewership Change Season-to-Season",
  x = "season number",
  y = "viewership change (million)"
) + theme_bw(
  base_size = 18
) + theme(
  plot.title = element_text(
    size = 22,
    face = "bold"
  ),
  plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 20),
  axis.title = element_text(size = 15)
)
```



### Observed Changes

From the last figure, I can see that, on average, $`{r} round(data_season$change[1], 3)`$ million more people watched the 2^nd^ season than the 1^st^ one. Then, the audience for the 3^rd^ season grew by an even greater number of $`{r} round(data_season$change[2], 3)`$ million people. Finally, the closing season was the most successful with an additional $`{r} round(data_season$change[2], 3)`$ million viewers.




## Disclaimer

During the preparation of this assignment, the author used

* [ChatGPT](https://chatgpt.com/) (v5.2) for helping summarize the series and calculating the changes between seasons in `R` and
* [DeepL](https://www.deepl.com/) for checking grammar and vocabulary.

The author verified the content and edited the assignment text in accordance with its objectives, presents his own intellectual contribution, and assumes full responsibility for the final version of his assignment.




## Works Cited

::: {#refs}
:::
